insights.title: Attacking Intent
insights.body: Mexico created the clearer chances, generating 1.95 xG and putting 5 shots on target.

insights.title: Pressing Intensity
insights.body: Higher pressing intensity from Mexico (PPDA 10.1) disrupted South Africa's rhythm and forced errors in dangerous areas.

insights.title: Decisive Moments
insights.body: The margin was slim but Mexico's ability to capitalise on key moments proved the difference in a tightly contested match.

insights.title: Superior Attacking Output
insights.body: United States generated 2.9 xG, creating 4 big chances and consistently overloading the defensive line.

insights.title: Territorial Control
insights.body: Dominant possession (62.7%) and high pressing intensity (PPDA 7.7) cut off Paraguay's build-up at source.

insights.title: Clinical Finishing
insights.body: Shot conversion rate was decisive — United States turned pressure into goals while Paraguay struggled to create clear openings.

insights.title: Evenly Matched Midfield Battle
insights.body: Both sides cancelled each other out in a tightly contested midfield duel, with neither team able to establish clear dominance.

insights.title: Lack of Clinical Edge
insights.body: Despite creating chances (combined xG 3.1), both teams were wasteful in front of goal when it mattered most.

insights.title: Tactical Discipline
insights.body: Compact defensive structures from both sides made it difficult to break through — a draw was a fair reflection of the contest.

insights.title: Defensive Resilience
insights.body: Scotland absorbed sustained pressure, conceding territory but maintaining defensive shape through disciplined low-block organization.

insights.title: Counter-Attack Efficiency
insights.body: Despite lower possession, Scotland converted limited chances with clinical precision on the break.

insights.title: Set-Piece Threat
insights.body: Dead-ball situations proved a key differentiator, with Scotland generating danger from corners and free-kicks throughout.
